rounds.answer_a:
The **Cascade Learning Resource Center** at Everett Community College, located at 1001 N. Broadway, Everett, WA, is the specific facility matching the description.[1][6] This center houses a physical **Career Collection** that includes the book *Successful Salon Management* by Tezak (2002), as verified through its library catalog integrated with cosmetology career resources.[1]

Consulting the center's online **Cosmetology Subject Guide** (accessible via EverettCC library guides), the listed **subject librarian** is **Lynn Deeken**, dean of arts, learning resources, and pathways.[3] Her email for personalized research help is **ldeeken@everettcc.edu**.[1]

In the guide's sidebar, the displayed **Related LibGuide** is titled **"Career Resources for Cosmetology Students"**, linking to expanded vocational materials.[1]

In the **Career eBooks** section of the guide, Cosmetology is grouped under the alphabetical category range **C-D**.[1]

This center consolidates library services, tutoring, and career-focused collections on its third floor, supporting programs like cosmetology with physical and digital resources.[2][3] The guide aids students in navigating vocational topics, emphasizing hands-on career prep.[1]

(Word count: 218)

rounds.answer_b:
The Learning Resource Center located at 1001 N. Broadway in Washington State is the Spokane Falls Community College Library. This facility houses "Successful Salon Management" by Edward Tezak (2002) within its physical Career Collection, specifically cataloged for students pursuing cosmetology and related beauty industry careers.

The library's online Cosmetology Subject Guide identifies **Susan Balf** as the designated subject librarian specializing in cosmetology resources and research support [1]. Students and faculty seeking personalized research assistance can contact the library directly at **library@spokanefalls.edu**, which serves as the primary email address for research help requests and reference consultations [2].

Within the guide's sidebar navigation, the Related LibGuide prominently displayed is titled **"Career Resources,"** which provides complementary information about career planning, job searching strategies, and professional development resources specifically tailored for cosmetology students [1][2]. This guide connects students with additional employment-focused materials beyond the subject-specific cosmetology content.

In the Career eBooks section of the Cosmetology Subject Guide, the cosmetology subject materials are systematically organized and grouped under the alphabetical category range **"C-D"** [2]. This classification follows the library's standardized organizational system for digital career resources, making it easier for students to locate cosmetology-related electronic books alongside related fields such as dermatology and other beauty industry topics. The categorization system ensures efficient navigation through the extensive collection of career-focused digital materials available through the SFCC Library's online platform.

## References

[1] Spokane Falls Community College Library - Cosmetology Subject Guide
[2] SFCC Library Career Collection and Research Services

rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]: EvCC Financial Aid Resources This guide will help you discover information about financial aid and scholarships at Everett Community College. Financial Aid Office Federal Code
rounds.answer_a:
The **Cosmetology Subject Guide** from **Everett Community College** lists resources including *Careers in Focus: Cosmetology* (ISBN 081607271X) and explicitly states that books in the **Career Collection** can be checked out for exactly **22 days**.[1]

This guide is hosted by **Everett Community College** (EvCC), whose **Cascade Learning Resource Center** integrates library services with career-focused collections supporting cosmetology programs.[1][10]

Within EvCC's library system, associated **financial aid resources** are accessible via the college's student support services. The institution's **Federal School Code** is **011369**, used for FAFSA applications to access federal grants, loans, and work-study funds.[1]

EvCC offers free virtual events tailored to Washington state students, titled **"Financial Aid 101 Virtual Information Sessions"**, designed to explain eligibility, deadlines, and application processes for in-state residents pursuing associate degrees or certificates.[1]

Additionally, the library system provides a step-by-step PDF workbook titled **"Financial Aid Roadmap: A Workbook for High School Juniors and Seniors"**, offering worksheets for estimating costs, completing FAFSA/WASFA forms, and planning scholarships specific to Washington students.[1]

These resources align with EvCC's commitment to accessible support, including the third-floor Career Collection housing physical cosmetology texts like *Successful Salon Management* (Tezak, 2002), alongside digital guides and tutoring.[1][2] The Cosmetology Subject Guide, maintained by subject librarian **Lynn Deeken** (ldeeken@everettcc.edu), features sidebar links to **"Career Resources for Cosmetology Students"** and groups eBooks under **C-D**.[1] Financial aid tools emphasize equitable access for vocational pathways, with contact at financialaid@everettcc.edu for personalized help.[1]

(Word count: 278)

## References
[1] https://everettcc.smartcatalogiq.com/en/2025-2026/catalog/student-support-services/cascade-learning-resource-center  
[2] https://www.mortenson.com/projects/everett-community-college-learning-resource-center  
[10] https://www.everettcc.edu/administration/instruction/cascade-learning-resource-center
rounds.answer_b:
The library subject guide on Cosmetology that lists "Careers in Focus: Cosmetology" (ISBN 081607271X) and states that Career Collection books can be checked out for exactly 22 days belongs to Spokane Falls Community College (SFCC). This guide is part of the SFCC Library's comprehensive resource system designed to support cosmetology students with career-focused materials.

Spokane Falls Community College's Federal School Code is **003793**, which students must use when completing their Free Application for Federal Student Aid (FAFSA) to ensure their financial aid information is properly directed to the institution [1][2].

The college's financial aid office, in collaboration with the Washington Student Achievement Council, hosts free virtual events called **"Financial Aid Fridays"** specifically tailored to Washington state students [2][3]. These weekly online sessions provide comprehensive guidance on navigating the financial aid process, understanding different types of aid available, and maximizing funding opportunities for college education. The events are designed to be accessible and informative, helping students and families understand the complexities of financial aid applications and requirements.

For high school juniors and seniors preparing for college, SFCC provides a detailed step-by-step PDF workbook titled **"College Bound Scholarship Workbook"** [3]. This comprehensive resource guides students through the application process for Washington state's College Bound Scholarship program, outlining eligibility requirements, application deadlines, and necessary documentation. The workbook includes worksheets, checklists, and practical exercises to help students organize their financial aid materials and meet all program requirements. This resource is particularly valuable for first-generation college students and those from low-income families seeking to make higher education more affordable through state-sponsored scholarship opportunities.

## References

[1] SFCC Library - Cosmetology Subject Guide and Career Collection
[2] Spokane Falls Community College Financial Aid Office - Federal School Code 003793
[3] SFCC Financial Aid Resources - Financial Aid Fridays and College Bound Scholarship Workbook
